      "それは楽観的なメッセージが特徴です。",
      "それは停滞した平和プロセスへの批判が特徴です。"

IsA: It is a street art mural.

It is used for bringing happiness to viewers.
It is used for sparking discussion about peace.

It is used for conveying a message of peace to both sides of the conflict.

It is located on the Bethlehem wall.
It is located on a street named Frenkel in Tel Aviv.
BuiltIn: It was built in Tel Aviv.
BuiltBy: It was built by John Kiss.
It was built by John Kiss and Moodi Abdallah.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the artist's yearning for peace.
It originated in the artist's desire to express peace visually.
It originated in the artist's contemplation of the Arab-Israeli conflict.
It originated in the artist's inspiration from existing symbols of Palestine and Israel.

It was created by Palestinian artist Moodi Abdallah.

It was created in Tel Aviv, Israel.

It was published in Reform Magazine.

It was produced by John Kiss and Moodi Abdallah.
CausedBy: It is caused by the artist's desire to promote peace.
It is caused by the artist's criticism of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.
Affects: It affects the viewer's perception of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.
It affects the discussion around peace in the Middle East.
It affects the viewer by making them stop and think.
ConsistsOf: It consists of the figures Srulik and Handala embracing.
It consists of a visual message of peace.
It consists of iconic symbols of Palestine and Israel.
PartOf: It is part of the artist's oeuvre.
It is part of the street art landscape of Tel Aviv.
It is part of the artist's body of work on peace.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the genre of street art.
It belongs to the artist's collection of peace-themed works.
KnownFor: It is known for its depiction of Srulik and Handala embracing.
It is known for its message of peace between Israelis and Palestinians.
It is known for being a symbol of hope and aspiration.
It is known for its dual creation in Palestine and Israel.
It is known for its controversy and mixed criticism.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the artist John Kiss.
It is associated with the artist Moodi Abdallah.
It is associated with the symbols Srulik and Handala.
It is associated with peace activism.
It is associated with the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.
About: It is about peace between Israelis and Palestinians.
It is about the hope for reconciliation in the Middle East.
It is about the artist's yearning for peace.
It is about the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.
It is about the possibility of coexistence.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the symbols Srulik and Handala.
It was influenced by the work of Banksy.
It was influenced by the artist's experiences with peace activism.
It was influenced by the political climate of the Middle East.
It was influenced by the desire to create an iconic peace sign.
Influenced: It influenced other artists to repaint it.
It influenced the design of shirts and stickers.
It influenced the content of artistic films and invitations.
